terms.definition: The measurable loss of structural, moral, and institutional order in a society over time, analogised to thermodynamic entropy.
A five-pillar indicator set—Faith, Authority, Covenant, Trust, Stewardship—used to operationalise moral decline.
A discontinuous shift from one macro-state to another once a control parameter crosses a critical point (e.g., liquid to gas).
In information theory, the agent whose measurements give a system its informational state; dissolution implies loss of coherent agency.
Mapping state variables such as entropy and free energy from physics onto analogue variables in social systems.
A one-to-one correspondence between relational patterns in two formally distinct domains.
Data collected on the same variables repeatedly over time, allowing trend detection.
Capable of being empirically disproven, following Popperian scientific standards.
